blocks.content: The debate ended at 7 p.m.
As the evening party hosted by the Teachers' Association began, the fervor of the discussion vanished as if it had never existed.
The attendees were busy sipping wine and exchanging conversations with notable figures from various fields.
Around Reina, high-ranking individuals had gathered—people whose names even those uninterested in the Teachers' Association would have heard.
*So this is what they mean when they say beauty is a burden.*
Sade waited for his turn to speak, eavesdropping on their conversation. Most of their words were either unbearably authoritative or teasing jokes.
Reina maintained a warm smile and responded kindly. This was unexpected for Sade. Her bright personality didn't match the infamous reputation of the 99th Council.
Art was a technique for handling emotions, and its power to captivate the masses was something even those in authority couldn't ignore.
Given art's critical nature, many artists were stubborn individuals. But Reina, as if completely detached from such rumors, mingled cheerfully with people from all walks of life.
*Haah, if only Ms. Siena had even half of her personality…*
As he sipped his wine, Reina finished her conversation and walked toward him.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Hello, Teacher Sade.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Ah, yes! Huh? Do you know me?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Of course. You're a teacher at Alpheas Magic School. The Ozent family's main house is in Creas.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Aha! That's right. I forgot for a moment. Nice to meet you.
Delighted to meet someone from their hometown, the two talked for a long time. As Sade had expected, Reina was bright and fun to be around.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: By the way, how is Shirone doing?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Huh? How do you know Shirone? Ah, right.
Sade, who had been the grading teacher for Shirone's entrance exam, recalled that Shirone had listed the Ozent family as his origin.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Shirone was a guest of the Ozent family. As expected of a prestigious family, your discernment is exceptional. Shirone is doing well. Though he did cause some trouble recently.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Huh? Trouble? What kind?
When Reina's eyes widened, Sade realized his mistake. If she found out that a student backed by the Ozent family had been suspended, her pride would be hurt.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Ahaha! It's nothing serious. He's such an outstanding student that even minor incidents become big news. That's all.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Ah, I see.
Reina sighed in relief. Just from Sade's words, she could guess how Shirone was living.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: I'm glad he's doing well. Does he have many friends?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Of course. He's popular with both boys and girls. Sometimes I'm even jealous. Hahaha!

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Oh? Then, perhaps…
Reina shook her head, stopping herself from asking if he had a lover. As a sponsor, it was a question she could ask. But the questioner in her heart wasn't borrowing the sponsor's name.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Huh? Is there something else you're curious about?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: No. I'm just glad he's doing well.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Haha! Don't worry. It's a bit presumptuous of me to say this, but Alpheas Magic School is a prestigious institution. You can rest assured.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Hehe, that's true. I was being presumptuous. Sorry.
Not at all. If anything, I was the rude one. How about joining me on the way to Creas? We're leaving in the morning. I'll treat you to a splendid lunch.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I have a performance schedule, so I need to return to Vashka.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Ah, I see. That's too bad…
Sade couldn't hide his disappointment. Normally, he'd let it go without a second thought, but she was someone he truly didn't want to part with.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: It can't be helped. But I'd love to see your performance. Could you tell me the schedule? I'll come to Vashka.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Oh, I'd be honored. Actually, I was worried. With the recent trouble in the capital, I was afraid there'd be too many empty seats.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Ah, what happened in Vashka?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: A dark sorcerer attacked Inferno and helped a B-class wanted criminal escape. The capital's security is almost under martial law now.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Dark… sorcerer?
Sade's brow furrowed. Among countless news stories, this one stood out as particularly unusual.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Dark sorcerers were rare in this day and age. And to attack Inferno, of all places? Just to free one B-class criminal?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: A scene from history flashed through Sade's mind.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Could you tell me more details?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: The incident happened a week ago. The escaped prisoner is a man named Lucas, formerly the vice-captain of the Parrot Thieves. The captain is still wanted.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Do you know anything about the person who attacked Inferno? Their appearance, perhaps?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Hmm, I'm not sure. I only heard rumors. An elderly man with white hair, I think. He had two attendants. They used dark magic to free the prisoners. Something about darkness… what was it?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: The Authority of Darkness?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Yes, that's it! As expected of a magic school teacher.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Reina's praise fell on deaf ears. Dark magic—and a high-level technique like the Authority of Darkness—used by an elderly man to attack Inferno.
*No way…*
Sade's head snapped up.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Sorry. I need to go.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Huh? Ah, okay.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: His politeness was almost instinctive. Sade no longer saw the party or the beautiful woman before him.
*Damn it! Of all times, when I'm away on business!*
The escape had happened a week ago. If they moved nonstop from Vashka, they'd have had enough time to reach Creas.
Carriage! A carriage!
Leaving the mansion, Sade jumped into the first carriage he saw. He opened the window to the driver's seat and shouted, To Creas! As fast as possible!

blocks.speaker: Driver
blocks.content: S-sir, all carriages here are reserved. If you're not on the list, I can't…
These carriages were meant for high-ranking officials, and no ordinary sum would sway the driver.
Sade rummaged through his pockets and pulled out all the money he had.

blocks.speaker: Driver
blocks.content: Here! Take it. Are you going or not?
The driver counted the gold coins scattered on the box seat and immediately changed his expression, preparing to depart.

blocks.speaker: Driver
blocks.content: We'll leave at once. We'll go through the night?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: No sleep, no breaks! Just go fast!
The sound of hooves clattered against the night air.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: *Damn it! I can't be late.*

Sade leaned back and pressed his forehead. He wanted to use spatial teleportation, but jumping repeatedly in an unfamiliar place without knowing the coordinates or environment would be suicidal, even with ten lives.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: The helplessness brought him back to his senses. As he assessed the situation, doubts arose.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: What's the plan behind his return?
Sade had never met or seen him. But the terrifying connection he'd heard from Alpheas was vivid in his memory.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Viltor Arcane…
Sade's eyes burned as he stared ahead.
Past 4 a.m., Kanis and his group entered the training ground on the mountainside of Alpheas Magic School.
Though they'd never attended the school, they could tell it was a top-tier facility, befitting the disciples of the Grand Sorcerer.
A frivolous voice broke the silence.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Wow, this is well-built. Looks like it's covered in gold coins. How much is the land worth?
Kanis frowned and turned his head. Lucas, the B-class wanted criminal, had a pair of swords with parrot engravings slung over his shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Keep it down. The guards might be patrolling.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: What's there to worry about? We can just cut them down. Or bury them.
Kanis's mood worsened. Though they were traveling together under their master's orders, he found nothing likable about the man.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Don't get it twisted. You're hired help. If you disrupt the plan, you'll be the first to go.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Ooh, scary. Kids these days are lucky. Meeting a great master and talking back to adults.
Lucas flopped onto the training ground. Since he didn't interfere further, Kanis suppressed his anger.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: The most important thing is Master's revenge. No matter how much I dislike him, I have to endure.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: By the way, why are we waiting here? We'll have to go down to the school anyway.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Master is going to cast a spell. It's a wide-area spell, so if we're in range, we won't survive either. If you're confident, you can go down first.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Heh, don't be so angry, little girl. Your cute face is getting ugly.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Hmph, I don't care how others look.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Don't be like that. How about dating this old man? I can teach you something more fun than magic.
As Arin's face turned red and she prepared to yell, Kanis cut in.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Quiet. It's starting.
The group looked up. Arcane was floating in the center of the moon, using Fly Magic.

Fly Magic was a fusion of two branches of Air Magic: Press and Blow. First, Press Magic created a solid air pressure around the caster, then Blow Magic generated wind to produce lift. Classified as overpowered due to its flight capability, it required delicate control as the wind changed in real time.
As darkness gathered, covering the moon, Arcane's eyes snapped open. Combining omniscience and omnipotence, he spread his limbs and cast his life's masterpiece: Abyss Nova.
There was no deafening noise or terrifying aftermath, but the sight of the school being engulfed in dark energy was spectacular.
As the effects of Abyss Nova spread beyond the school and faded, the moon reappeared, brightening the surroundings.
Hah… Hah…
Arcane gasped for breath. Even for a Grand Sorcerer, this spell consumed 90% of his mental energy.

blocks.speaker: Arcane
blocks.content: It'll be hard to recover for a while.
He turned toward the mountain. Maintaining Fly Magic was already difficult, but his anger burned stronger.

blocks.speaker: Arcane
blocks.content: With Abyss Nova spread, no one could stop him. And hadn't he raised disciples precisely for this situation?

blocks.speaker: Arcane
blocks.content: Hehehe, just wait, Alpheas. I'll make you taste the humiliation I suffered 40 years ago.
The dark night passed, and a faint light rose from the eastern foothills.
